name: ProtectionGroupSnapshot
description:
A protection group snapshot is a collection of point-in-time consistent volume
snapshots, for volumes associated with the protection group when the snapshot
was taken.

allOf.description:
A built-in resource. Many are singletons predefined by Purity (e.g., support
settings). Some correspond to a piece of software, like an app, or hardware,
like a controller. Others are created by the system in response to some event
(e.g., alerts, audit records).

Typically, a user can't create, delete or rename a built-in resource. A few
can be created or deleted, but not renamed because the names are meaningful
to Purity (e.g., VIFs).

id.description: A non-modifiable, globally unique ID chosen by the system.

name.description: A non-modifiable, locally unique name chosen by the system.

created.description: Creation time in milliseconds since UNIX epoch.

on.description: A reference to the array where the snapshot is stored.
on.title: FixedReferenceFqdn

pod.description: A reference to the pod the source protection group belongs to, if applicable.
pod.title: FixedReference

source.description: A reference to the protection group that the snapshot was taken from.
source.title: FixedReference
